bead.title: Avoid double decoding Fetch requests on the hot path
bead.description:
Fetch requests currently take an avoidable double-decode path. Router::route_async decodes the request header and then handle_fetch_long_poll decodes the FetchRequest to inspect max_wait_ms and requested offsets. When data is available it calls route(data), which decodes the header again and fetch::handle decodes the FetchRequest again. This is cheap per request but visible when consume issues many fetches. Refactor the fetch route so the decoded header/body and, where possible, decoded FetchRequest metadata are reused instead of decoding the same frame multiple times. Preserve max_wait_ms long-poll behavior and existing wire compatibility.

In-scope files:
- crates/heimq/src/protocol/router.rs
- crates/heimq/src/handler/fetch.rs
- crates/heimq/src/handler/tests.rs or existing focused tests, if needed
- crates/heimq/tests/bench_baseline.rs only if needed to keep diagnostics compiling

Out-of-scope:
- Changing Kafka Fetch response semantics.
- Storage layout or copy-reduction work.
- Consumer group rebalance behavior.
bead.acceptance:
1. `cargo test -p heimq fetch --lib` passes.
2. `cargo test -p heimq --test integration test_rdkafka_produce_consume_roundtrip -- --ignored --nocapture` passes.
3. `BENCH_RECORDS=1000 BENCH_LATENCY_SAMPLES=10 BENCH_DIAGNOSTICS=1 cargo test -p heimq --release --test bench_baseline -- --ignored --nocapture` passes and still reports consume diagnostics.
